Description: Homepage loads successfully and is interactive
Preconditions: Guest user; staging URL is reachable
Steps: 1. Open homepage URL 2. Wait for DOM ready 3. Dismiss any interruption (cookie/geo/modal)
Expected Result: Page loads over HTTPS without crash, application error UI, or empty body. Brand logo and main navigation are visible after interruptions dismissed.
Notes: Consolidates v1 HP-001/003/004/005/046/047 (all variations of "page loads ok"). One critical assertion is enough.

Description: Header essential entry points are present
Steps: 1. Observe header
Expected Result: Logo, primary navigation, search entry point, account entry point, and cart entry point are all visible and enabled.
Notes: Single TC verifying all header surface — replaces 5 separate v1 entries

Description: Search with valid keyword navigates to results page
Preconditions: Homepage loaded; brand has a known good search keyword
Steps: 1. Open search input 2. Enter keyword from searchData[brand] 3. Submit
Expected Result: URL changes to a search-results pattern (matches /search|q=|query=|\/s\//) and the results page contains the searched keyword.
Notes: Smoke path — depends on testData being valid per brand

Description: Clicking logo from any page returns to homepage
Preconditions: Homepage loaded then navigate to any subpage
Steps: 1. Click logo from a non-homepage page
Expected Result: URL pathname becomes '/' and homepage layout is restored.

Description: Footer is rendered with legal text
Steps: 1. Scroll to bottom
Expected Result: Footer container is visible. Footer text contains at least one of: "copyright", "all rights reserved", "terms", "privacy", or copyright symbol.

Description: Top-level navigation items load PLPs without error
Steps: 1. Capture all top-nav item hrefs 2. Click each (or first 3 for speed)
Expected Result: Each navigated URL pathname matches the captured href. Destination page renders without application error UI.
Notes: Limit to first 3 items to avoid runtime blow-up

Description: Submenu opens on hover for desktop nav items with subnav
Preconditions: Desktop viewport; nav item with submenu exists
Steps: 1. Hover each visible nav item 2. Wait for submenu to appear
Expected Result: At least one nav item exposes a visible submenu (`[role=menu]` or known submenu container).
Notes: Brand without any submenu should skip with reason — not fail

Description: Hero banner is rendered with image and clickable CTA
Steps: 1. Observe hero section 2. Locate hero CTA
Expected Result: Hero banner element is visible. Hero CTA has a non-empty href.
Notes: Don't assert image dimensions — let visual regression handle that

Description: Hero CTA navigates to the URL declared in its href
Preconditions: Hero CTA exists with valid href
Steps: 1. Click hero CTA
Expected Result: URL pathname after navigation matches href pathname.
Notes: Partial because hero content is CMS-driven — href may legitimately be a 3rd-party tracker URL that redirects; skip with reason if href hostname differs

Description: Promotional carousel can be navigated manually via next/prev controls
Steps: 1. Capture carousel signature (visible slide text + image src) 2. Click next 3. Wait 1s 4. Capture signature again 5. Click prev 6. Wait 1s 7. Capture signature
Expected Result: Signature changes after next click; signature changes again (and differs from after-next) after prev click.
Notes: Auto-rotation TC (v1 HP-020) removed — flaky and brand-dependent timing. Manual navigation is the deterministic part.

Description: Promotional tile CTAs navigate correctly
Steps: 1. Capture first 2 promo tile hrefs 2. Click each (with re-navigate between)
Expected Result: Each click navigates to URL pathname matching the captured href.

Description: Featured product module renders product cards with required elements
Steps: 1. Scroll to featured products 2. Inspect first card
Expected Result: Card has visible image, non-empty product name, and at least one price element.

Description: Clicking featured product card navigates to PDP
Steps: 1. Click first product card
Expected Result: URL pathname matches the card's href. Destination renders without application error.

Description: Featured product price uses correct currency format for region
Steps: 1. Read price text from first 3 cards
Expected Result: All prices match pattern `^\$\s?\d{1,3}(,\d{3})*(\.\d{1,2})?$`. No mixed currency symbols (e.g. no `£`, `€`).
Notes: Region-specific currency value validated in HP-AU-001/HP-NZ-001

Description: Footer internal links navigate without error
Steps: 1. Pick first 2 visible internal footer links (same hostname) 2. Click each
Expected Result: Each navigates to its declared pathname without 404/500/error UI.
Notes: Exclude social and tel:/mailto: links

Description: Search with invalid keyword shows no-results state
Steps: 1. Open search 2. Enter unique invalid keyword (e.g. `no-results-{timestamp}-zzzxxy`) 3. Submit
Expected Result: Page navigates to search URL. Body contains the invalid keyword. Body matches no-results pattern (/no results|0 results|couldn't find|did not match/i).

Description: Cart icon shows guest empty state
Steps: 1. Inspect cart icon
Expected Result: Cart icon visible. Aria-label or text content includes one of: "cart", "bag", "basket", "0", or "empty".
Notes: Bag/Cart wording difference validated more strictly in HP-drm-001

Description: Mobile hamburger menu opens and shows nav links
Steps: 1. Open homepage 2. Tap hamburger menu button
Expected Result: Mobile menu surface becomes visible with at least one nav link inside.
Notes: If hamburger not detectable for the brand → skip with reason

Description: Mobile navigation link navigates correctly
Steps: 1. Pick first visible mobile nav link with valid href 2. Click
Expected Result: URL pathname matches the link href.

Description: Tablet viewport renders main and header without horizontal overflow
Steps: 1. Open homepage
Expected Result: Main element visible. Header logo visible. documentElement.scrollWidth - clientWidth <= 1 (no horizontal overflow).

Description: Mobile viewport renders without horizontal overflow
Steps: 1. Open homepage
Expected Result: Same as HP-020 but at mobile width.

Description: Sticky header remains in viewport after scroll
Steps: 1. Read header position 2. Scroll down 1.5× viewport 3. Read header position again
Expected Result: Header has computed position sticky or fixed. After-scroll header top differs from initial by less than 6px.
Notes: Skip with reason if brand opts out of sticky header

Description: Featured products include at least one sale product with both prices shown
Preconditions: Featured products visible; sale items exist on staging
Steps: 1. Inspect first 8 cards 2. Find card with ≥2 distinct price strings
Expected Result: At least one card has min(prices) < max(prices). If none found → skip with @data-dependent reason.
Notes: @data-dependent — sale items may not exist on every run. Partial because we cannot guarantee a sale card exists on every visit.

Description: Social footer links point to expected external host
Steps: 1. Pick first 2 social-domain links 2. Click each (handle popup)
Expected Result: Resulting page hostname contains one of: facebook|instagram|tiktok|youtube|pinterest|x.com|twitter|linkedin.
Notes: Open in new tab — handle popup event; close popup after assertion

Description: Category click fires a GTM dataLayer event
Steps: 1. Wrap dataLayer.push to capture events 2. Click first category link 3. Read captured delta
Expected Result: At least one event captured. Event payload (serialized) matches /category|navigation|menu|select|click/i.
Notes: Partial — we verify event fires, not exact payload schema. Replaces v1 HP-049/050/051/052 which all attempted analytics validation.

Description: Dr. Martens uses ""Bag"" terminology in cart icon (not ""Cart"" or ""Basket"")
Preconditions: Homepage loaded on a DM project
Steps: 1. Inspect cart icon aria-label and inner text
Expected Result: Combined aria-label + inner text contains case-insensitive "bag". Must NOT contain standalone "cart" or "basket" word.
Notes: Brand differentiator — DM is the only brand using "Add to Bag"/"Bag" terminology

Description: Dr. Martens shows OneTrust cookie banner on first visit and it can be dismissed
Preconditions: Fresh browser context (no cookies); DM project
Steps: 1. Open homepage with clean context 2. Wait for `#onetrust-banner-sdk` to appear 3. Click accept-all button
Expected Result: OneTrust banner appears within 5s. After click, banner element is hidden or removed. Page is interactive.
Notes: Applies to DM/Platypus/Skechers — could be promoted to shared TC if other brands need it

Description: Vans cookie banner is NOT OneTrust (CMS-injected)
Steps: 1. Open homepage 2. Wait for cookie banner candidate to appear
Expected Result: `#onetrust-banner-sdk` element does NOT exist. Some other cookie-consent element (with text matching /cookie|consent|accept/i) is present.
Notes: Documents intentional difference — Vans injects via CMS design_head_includes

Description: Clicking account icon opens a modal popup (does not navigate to /account)
Steps: 1. Click account/login icon in header
Expected Result: URL pathname remains '/' (no navigation). A dialog element ([role="dialog"] or [aria-modal="true"]) becomes visible within 3s.
Notes: Vans-specific UX pattern — all other brands page-navigate

Description: Guest can add a product to wishlist; entry persists in localStorage
Preconditions: Guest user; Vans project; PLP accessible from homepage
Steps: 1. Open homepage 2. Click a category to PLP 3. Click wishlist icon on first product card 4. Read localStorage
Expected Result: At least one localStorage key contains a wishlist payload referencing the product (key matches /wishlist|favorite|saved/i and value contains product identifier).
Notes: Vans is the only brand supporting guest wishlist

Description: Featured products render only after SPA hydration (waitForFunction polling required)
Steps: 1. Open homepage with `waitUntil: "domcontentloaded"` 2. Immediately count product cards 3. Use waitForFunction to poll until product cards > 0 4. Re-count
Expected Result: Product card count at domcontentloaded should be 0 (or smaller than after polling). After polling completes, product cards > 0.
Notes: Validates the SPA-hydration assumption documented in CLAUDE.md. If domcontentloaded already has cards, the assumption is wrong and selectors should be reviewed.

Description: Skechers mini-cart does NOT show Afterpay messaging (brand feature flag)
Preconditions: Logged-in or guest with 1 item in cart; Skechers project
Steps: 1. Add a product 2. Open mini-cart 3. Inspect mini-cart body text
Expected Result: Mini-cart body text does NOT contain /afterpay|after pay|installments|4 payments/i.
Notes: Cross-spec dependency — actual TC may move to mini-cart spec

Description: Featured product link from homepage uses root-level URL pattern (not /shop/)
Steps: 1. Inspect first featured product card href
Expected Result: Href pathname matches /^\/[^/]+-\d+-[^/]+\.html$/ (root-level slug-sku-colour.html). Does NOT start with /shop/.
Notes: Platypus uses flat PDP URLs unlike DM and Skechers

Description: Platypus has a discoverable entry point to Kickbacks loyalty program
Steps: 1. Scroll through full homepage including footer 2. Search anchors for href containing 'kickbacks' or text 'Kickbacks'
Expected Result: At least one anchor with href containing 'kickbacks' OR text content matching /kickbacks/i is present and visible.
Notes: Reference: /platypus-kickbacks-terms-conditions page exists

Description: AU homepage prices use AUD context (no NZ markers)
Steps: 1. Read first 5 featured product prices and surrounding text
Expected Result: Prices match $XX.XX format. Surrounding text/copy does NOT contain 'NZD' or 'NZ$' tokens. Hostname contains '-au.'.

Description: NZ homepage prices use NZD context
Steps: 1. Read first 5 featured product prices and surrounding text 2. Read hostname
Expected Result: Prices match $XX.XX format. Hostname contains '-nz.'. Body text contains at least one NZ-specific marker (e.g. 'NZD', 'NZ$', 'New Zealand', or NZ-only category text).

Description: Platypus NZ has Mens ""New Arrivals"" in top navigation (AU does not)
Steps: 1. Hover Mens nav 2. Read submenu items
Expected Result: Submenu contains an item with text matching /new arrivals/i.
Notes: NZ-only nav structure per site-structure.md

Description: Vans NZ exposes droplist entry point
Steps: 1. Scan homepage links (header, body, footer) for href '/droplist'
Expected Result: At least one anchor with href ending '/droplist' is present.
Notes: Documents NZ-only feature

Description: Vans AU homepage loads with graceful degradation (site is currently under construction)
Steps: 1. Open homepage 2. Verify minimum surface only
Expected Result: Homepage URL responds with 2xx. Logo is visible. No application error UI shown. Lower expectations than other brands — featured products / promo tiles may be absent.
Test Data: Vans-AU sitemap has 0 PDPs as of 2026-05-15
Notes: Documents temporary state. When AU site re-launches with full content, update this TC or remove.
